drills.name: Circle tracking quarter-cuts
drills.description: Cut the circle in four controlled quadrants while maintaining traction, pausing between each quarter to verify alignment with the marked line.
drills.targets_weakness: Cut outside the marked boundary lines in multiple locations

drills.name: Traction-angle practice
drills.description: 10 repetitions of repositioning the Maryland to improve scissor entry angle and maintain optimal cutting position throughout the circle.
drills.targets_weakness: Inconsistent precision when following the circular path

drills.name: Edge-entry rehearsal
drills.description: 15 starts from the gauze edge to a marked line without overshoot, focusing on controlled entry and immediate path correction.
drills.targets_weakness: Cut outside the marked boundary lines in multiple locations

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your consistent traction technique into more precise line-following accuracy. Your completion shows good fundamentals but needs refinement in boundary control to achieve faster, cleaner cuts.
